examples: Around the year 1000 A.D., some people from Northwest India began to travel westwards.
He sees that warm water flows back eastwards when winds that normally blow westwards weaken, or sometimes the other way round.
The trading and seafaring skills of the Phoenicians resulted in a network of colonies, spreading westwards through the Mediterranean.
As his forces moved westwards almost unhindered, he was acclaimed as the country's saviour.
Brazilians, once described as clinging to the coast like crabs, have scurried westwards and northwards.
